entries.definition:
كتاب السّين

سوس - سيب

(سير) السين والياء والراء أصلٌ يدلُّ على مضيٍّ وجَرَيان، يقال سار يسير سيراً، وذلك يكونُ ليلاً ونهاراً. والسِّيرة: الطَّريقة في الشيء والسُّنّة، لأنَّها تسير وتجري. يقال سارت، وسِرْتُها أنا. قال:

s-y-r to march, to walk, to travel, to journey, to sail, caravan; common; manner of ruling other people; biographies; straps. Of this root, six forms occur 27 times in the Qurʾan: سار sāra 16 times; يُسَيِّرُ yusayyir twice; سُيِّرَ suyyira three times; سير sayr twice; سيرة sīratun once and سيارة sayyāratun three times.
472 س/ي/ل

سار sāra i [v. intrans.] 1 to travel (6:11) سِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ travel throughout the earth 2 to move along (52:10) وَتَسِيرُ الْجِبَالُ سَيْرًا and the mountains move [with a definite] movement.

يُسَيِّرُ yusayyir [imperf. of v. II, trans.; pass. سُيِّرَ suyyira] 1 to cause to move along, to set in motion (18:47) وَيَوْمَ نُسَيِّرُ الْجِبَالَ on the day We set the mountains in motion 2 to enable to travel (10:22) هُوَ الَّذِي يُسَيِّرُكُمْ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ He it is who enables you to travel on land and sea 3 to be set in motion (78:20) وَسُيِّرَتِ الْجِبَالُ فَكَانَتْ سَرَابًا when the mountains are set in motion and become like a mirage (or, and become a moving object).

سَيْر sayr I [n.] travel, a distance measured in the time it takes to travel it, a stage (34:18) وَقَدَّرْنَا فِيهَا السَّيْرَ and we well measured travelling [time/distance] between them [to make travelling convenient between the villages] II [v. n. used adverbially for emphasis] moving (52:10) وَتَسِيرُ الْجِبَالُ سَيْرًا and the mountains move [with a definite] movement.

سِيرَة sīratun [n.] state, condition, way (20:21) سَنُعِيدُهَا سِيرَتَهَا الْأُولَى We will restore it to its former state.

سَيَّارَة sayyāratun [n.] caravan, company of travellers through the desert (12:19) وَجَاءَتْ سَيَّارَةٌ فَأَرْسَلُوا وَارِدَهُمْ فَأَدْلَى دَلْوَهُ travellers came by, and they sent their water fetcher and he let down his bucket.
